trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation:
Hunter Williams (2026-03-30, GHRH vs GHRP: How to Stack Growth Hormone Peptides, 00:03:55, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/ghrh-vs-ghrp-how-to-stack-growth-hormone-peptides--2bcd2680/): "The reasons that sermorelin is not in the cheatsheet is that we have things that are better that the same price or cheaper, then you would get sermarelin for it."
Hunter Williams (2026-04-07, These Peptides Are Overrated (Here's What Actually Works), 00:10:51, https://youtu.be/ODWpYYPTB_8): "But as it stands now, we have things that are so much better than ceremerelin."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
Hunter Williams (2026-04-07, These Peptides Are Overrated (Here's What Actually Works), 00:11:04, https://youtu.be/ODWpYYPTB_8): "It does stimulate GH release and has clinical data, but it has a very short half-life and it produces a brief relatively weak GH pulse."
Hunter Williams (2026-04-07, These Peptides Are Overrated (Here's What Actually Works), 00:11:29, https://youtu.be/ODWpYYPTB_8): "Many users report underwhelming results even after months of consistent use. It also appears to lose efficacy over time."
Dr Trevor Bachmeyer (2025-10-30, What is Tesamorelin - Unbreakable Podcast 296, 00:14:58, https://youtu.be/Fj9_wLXso1c): "Never mind the cortisol and the prolactin spike that sermorelin causes all the time."
Hunter Williams (2026-04-20, The FDA Just Changed Everything for Peptides | My Honest Take, 00:04:57, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/the-fda-just-changed-everything-for-peptides-my-honest-take--4ffa970d/): "Ceramorelin, I believe, is a Category 1 Peptide. Which means that it's not FDA approved, although it was at one time. It no longer is FDA-approved."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement:
Hunter Williams (2026-04-07, These Peptides Are Overrated (Here's What Actually Works), 00:11:59, https://youtu.be/ODWpYYPTB_8): "I will say it probably does work for some people, but I just think like, again, relative to what we have access to, why would you use it?"
Taylor Williams (2026-03-14, Saturday Morning Coffee Talk 3/14/2026, 01:15:31, https://youtu.be/JC1poI1OEog): "Would it be overkill to do sermorelin and Ipamorelin on the same night? No, I don't think so at all. It would be no different than doing Ipamorelin or Tesamorelin or HGH. I think it's fine."
Dr Trevor Bachmeyer (2025-10-30, What is Tesamorelin - Unbreakable Podcast 296, 00:14:34, https://youtu.be/Fj9_wLXso1c): "Forget it. Sermorelin is a 29 amino acid fragment. It is the obsolete eight-track player stereotype."

chronic.dose: Hunter Williams (2026-03-30, Peptide Q&A Â· January 2026, library article, timestamp unavailable, https://library.hunterwilliamshealth.com/p/peptide-qa-january-2026--7994649b/): "If you want Sermorelin, 200 to 400 micrograms is where most people land."
